# Test that generated code compiles and can be used
# This ensures the generated Go code is syntactically valid

# Create a tool for testing
-- compile_test_tool.json --
{
  "result": {
    "tools": [
      {
        "name": "Task",
        "description": "Launch a new task",
        "inputSchema": {
          "type": "object",
          "properties": {
            "description": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "A short (3-5 word) description of the task"
            },
            "prompt": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "The task for the agent to perform"
            }
          },
          "required": ["description", "prompt"],
          "additionalProperties": false,
          "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#"
        }
      }
    ]
  },
  "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  "id": 1
}

exec go run .. < compile_test_tool.json
cp stdout generated.go

# The output should have valid Go syntax
stdout 'package main'
stdout 'type TaskTool interface'
stdout 'type TaskInput struct'
stdout 'Execute\(ctx context.Context'

# Verify that required imports are present
stdout 'import \('
stdout '"context"'

# Verify structure fields have proper types and tags
stdout 'Description string.*json:"description"'
stdout 'Prompt string.*json:"prompt"'
